question: The scatter plot shows CO₂ emissions index vs. temperature anomaly. Which statement is best supported by the data?

options.A: Higher CO₂ emission index values generally correspond to lower temperature anomalies.
options.B: Higher CO₂ emission index values generally correspond to higher temperature anomalies.
options.C: There is no consistent relationship between CO₂ emissions and temperature anomaly.
options.D: The largest, darkest point indicates that very high CO₂ emissions always produce the highest temperature anomalies.

explanation: This scatter plot uses dual encoding: point position shows the relationship, but point size and color also encode the y-variable, making high values overly salient. That can tempt viewers to overstate the strength or form of the relationship.
